posts.text:
“Iran’s nuclear program exists because of CHINA.”

@GordonGChang says Beijing played a central role in enabling Tehran’s nuclear ambitions.

“There have been direct transfers of materials and technology from China to Iran. The United States needs to start calling China out for that proliferation.”

Video

“Democrats care more about DEFUNDING ICE than securing the border.”

@SenEricSchmitt says voters see through what he calls political games driven by Trump Derangement Syndrome.

“They let 15 million people into this country — including violent criminals and terrorists — and now they’re trying to defund the Department of Homeland Security.”

Video

“Iran’s pressure tactics are acts of TERRORISM.”

Kevin Donegan says Tehran is trying to threaten the global economy through attacks in the Strait of Hormuz.

“Opening the strait isn’t just in the U.S. interest — it’s in the global interest, and it will require working with allies and partners.”

Video

posts.text:
“Lone actors worry me more than sleeper cells.”

@dbongino says U.S. intelligence has powerful tools to detect coordinated terror activity.

“It’s very difficult to activate a terror cell in the United States without leaving huge investigative breadcrumbs. We’re watching, we’re listening.”

Video

Morgan Wright says early release policies can put Americans at risk.

“Anyone convicted of terrorism should serve their FULL sentence. Good-time credits shouldn’t apply to terrorists.”

Video

“We’re fighting the regime — not the Iranian people.”

@VDHanson says the U.S. is deliberately limiting targets to avoid harming civilians.

“We could do far more damage much faster. But we’re trying to demilitarize the regime without making life insufferable for the people we ultimately want to liberate.”

Video

“We’re degrading Iran’s ability to threaten ships.”

Energy @SecretaryWright says the U.S. military is steadily weakening Tehran’s capacity to disrupt the Strait of Hormuz.

“Every hour of every day we are degrading their military ability to threaten ships in the Straits of Hormuz — and ultimately ships will flow again.”

Video

posts.text:
Dr. @siegel.marc  warns that KRATOM may be a growing and underreported problem in the United States.

An estimated 15 million Americans could be using the substance, yet many cases may go untested and unreported in medical settings and overdose investigations. Siegel says the drug is widely available in everyday places like gas stations and convenience stores, making it easily accessible.

“I go in there for a Snickers bar and there’s ‘gas station heroin’. This has gotta be stopped right now.”
